message:
fix(dashboard): pin scroll position drifts after navigating back (#7944) (#7953)

The cleanup effect that persists a dashboard's scroll position on navigation
was overwriting the saved entry with `{ position: refScroll }`, which drops
the `cardTitle` hint that restoreScrollPosition relies on for layout-robust
restore. On the next visit, restoration falls back to the raw pixel value,
and because content above the card grid (StatsOverview, GettingStartedBanner,
DemoToLocalCTA, PostConnectBanner, etc.) lazy-loads after the header, the
pixel value drifts: each round-trip shows the pinned card in a different
position.

Three changes:

1. saveScrollPositionNow accepts an optional `scrollTopOverride` so callers
   (the pathname-change cleanup) can supply the real scroll position that
   the scroll handler captured before KeepAlive set display:none on the
   outgoing route. The override flows through the card-snap logic so the
   cardTitle is recomputed and stored.

2. The cleanup effect now routes through saveScrollPositionNow(path, refScroll)
   instead of writing a bare {position} entry — cardTitle is preserved.

3. When every card is hidden (KeepAlive already applied display:none before
   we got to run), getBoundingClientRect returns zeros and the snap picks
   nothing. In that case, fall back to the cardTitle already in storage
   rather than wiping it.

Also removes the maxCardsToCheck=12 cap in favour of a 100-card ceiling.
The cap was a premature optimisation: the loop already early-exits at the
first card past the viewport top, so the only iterations capped were the
degenerate "every card is above the fold" case (a dashboard scrolled past
its 12th card). On that dashboard the saved cardTitle was always card #11,
so even a 12-card dashboard couldn't remember positions past the middle.

Magic numbers in the snap logic (20px tolerance, 12px breathing room, 2px
row epsilon) are now named constants.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
